commercial.source_notes: Source{d} is a Madrid-based AI startup founded in 2015, focused on machine learning for large-scale analysis of developer code. It has raised $6M in funding, operates with a small team of 25 employees, and aligns with 'ai engineering', 'automation', 'agentic workflows', and 'software company' themes. Evidence from Nanalyze and Firma.dev confirms its focus on AI in development workflows, including code generation, testing, and documentation. The company meets all hard constraints (Spain-based, under 50 employees) and most search themes. The only gap is the absence of a named buyer persona (CEO, founder, CTO, etc.) in the available data.
commercial.fit_summary: Source{d} is a strong close match: Spain-based, under 50 employees, and deeply embedded in AI engineering, automation, and agentic workflows. It has raised $6M and is actively using AI in core development processes. While the preferred buyer persona isn’t yet identified, the company’s profile and evidence strongly support commercial relevance and alignment with the search criteria.
commercial.connection_note_draft: I came across Source{d} while researching Spain-based AI startups with small, agile teams focused on engineering innovation. Their work in machine learning for code analysis and AI-driven development workflows aligns closely with what we’re building. Even without a named contact, the company’s trajectory and focus make it a compelling opportunity to explore.
commercial.dm_draft:
Hey [First Name],

I came across Source{d}—a Madrid-based AI startup with 25 people, $6M in funding, and a sharp focus on AI engineering and automation in code workflows. They’re using AI for testing, documentation, and development speed, which mirrors what we’re seeing in high-velocity teams.

Even without a named contact, the fit feels strong. Would you be open to a quick intro if we can connect on a shared interest in AI-powered engineering?

Best,
[Your Name]
commercial.email_subject: Quick intro: Spain-based AI startup using AI to accelerate code workflows
commercial.email_body:
Hi [First Name],

I came across Source{d}—a Madrid-based AI startup with 25 employees and $6M in funding, focused on machine learning for developer code analysis. They’re using AI for testing, documentation, and development speed, which aligns closely with what we’re seeing in high-velocity engineering teams.

While we don’t yet have a named contact, the company’s profile and evidence from Firma.dev and Nanalyze suggest strong alignment with AI engineering and automation. If this resonates, I’d love to explore a quick intro.

Best,
[Your Name]

dossier.query_used: Spain startup AI engineering automation small team

evidence.title: Top-10 Artificial Intelligence Startups in Spain - Nanalyze
evidence.snippet: Founded in 2015, Madrid startup MedLab Media Group has raised **$11.2 million** in funding to develop a suite of products that assists the medical community in knowledge sharing and information flow. Founded in 2015, Madrid startup Geoblink has raised **$7.3 million** to create a geospatial analytics platform targeting retail, **F**ast **M**oving **C**onsumer **G**oods (**FMCG**), and real estate. Founded in 2014, Madrid startup seedtag has raised **$6 million** to develop an in-image advertising platform. Founded in 2015, Madrid startup source{d} has raised **$6 million**to develop machine learning algorithms for large scale analysis of developer code. ## vLex. Founded in 1998, Barcelona startup vLex has raised **$5.2 million** to develop a database of legal texts with multilingual search using NLP. Founded in 2014, Barcelona startup Spaceboost has raised **$4 million** to develop an automated **P**ay-**P**er-**C**lick (**PPC**) advertising platform for Google, Bing, and Amazon. Founded in 2014, Madrid startup MedUX has raised nearly **$4 million** in disclosed funding to develop a platform that measures quality of service and user experience for telecommunication operators.

evidence.title: We analyzed 100 AI-assisted startups: How small teams ... - Firma.dev
evidence.snippet: We reviewed 70 responses from early-stage teams using AI in their product and development workflows. * **AI definition:** use in planning, coding, testing, docs, release, support, or analytics. Teams reported meaningful drops in cycle time after adopting AI. Pattern in comments: AI plus API-first services compresses integration work so teams focus on business logic. A: Code generation (e.g., Copilot, Cursor, Replit AI), testing / QA automation, documentation or changelog writing, product design or prototyping, customer support automation, analytics / summarization, not currently using AI. 6. Q: Roughly how much faster is your team shipping with AI? A: No change, delayed or reduced hiring plans, reduced reliance on contractors, increased output per developer, started hiring AI-focused roles. A: Coding and implementation, testing & QA, documentation & knowledge sharing, release management, integration with third-party APIs, product or UX design. Q: If you could give one piece of advice to other teams adopting AI, what would it be?
